metadata.title:
CANADIAN ABSORBS THE


NORTH COAST RAILROAD

pages.raw_text:
city to supervise the location Of
the line to be constructed from
Sumas to Seattle. All Of the pre-
liminary surveys were completed,
and the route practically decided
before the negotiations for the
North Coast holdings were closed.


A large tract Of Lake Union Wa
terfront property has been quietly
purchased by the representatives Of
the Canadian Pacific, and practical-
ly all Of the details Of location OF
terminals and the route to be fol-
lowed in building the connecting
links Of the Canadian Pacific, that
will give the road access to Seattle
have been completed.


Within the next few weeks It Isl
expected the application Of the
North Coast railroad for fran
chise to enter this city will be re-
newed and the connection with the
Canadian road officially announced.


1t is the intention of the Cana
dian Pacific to have the line COM
pleted to Seattle and in operation
in time to participate in the direct
business to the Alaska Yukon-P Pa
cific exposition in 1909. With con-
siderable less mileage to build than
either the Milwaukee or the Union
Pacific to connect Seattle with itq
main line, the Canadian Pacific Will
make an effort to be the first Of
the new roads to enter this city
with its completed road.
